doctor_questions.text: What is the main goal or purpose of this clinical trial?
Why is this trial being recommended for my type and stage of cancer?
What potential benefits could I expect if I join this trial?
What are the most common and serious risks or side effects of this trial?
How often will I need to come to the clinic, and how long is each visit?
Will I be able to use telehealth or remote visits for any part of this trial?
What costs will my insurance cover, and what costs might I have to pay myself?
Is travel assistance or a stipend available to help with transportation and lodging?
What standard treatments or other trials are available to me if I do not join this study?
Can I leave the trial at any time, and what happens to my care if I withdraw?
¿Cuál es el objetivo principal de este estudio clínico?
¿Qué beneficios potenciales podría obtener si participo en este ensayo?
¿Cuáles son los riesgos y efectos secundarios más importantes que debo conocer?
¿Con qué frecuencia tendré que acudir a la clínica y se permiten visitas por telemedicina?

patient_resources.description: Detailed information for patients with HER2-positive metastatic breast cancer, including targeted therapies and clinical trials.

patient_resources.description: Guide for patients who have had surgery for Stage III colorectal cancer and are considering additional treatments or trials.

patient_resources.description: How to find and evaluate prostate cancer clinical trials in your area.

patient_resources.description: Respuestas a las dudas más comunes sobre la participación en ensayos clínicos de cáncer de colon.

patient_resources.description: Introducción en español a los conceptos principales sobre los ensayos clínicos oncológicos.

patient_resources.title: Completed Melanoma Trials: How to Find and Interpret Results
patient_resources.description: Step-by-step guide for locating and reading results from completed melanoma Phase III trials.

trials.title: Phase II Targeted Therapy Trial for Advanced Lung Cancer in Chicago
trials.brief_summary: A Phase II study evaluating a targeted therapy for adults with advanced non-small cell lung cancer.
trials.detailed_description: This single-arm Phase II study enrolls adults with Stage IIIB/IV non-small cell lung cancer. Participants will receive an oral targeted therapy and attend regular safety and response assessments at a downtown Chicago cancer center.
trials.inclusion_criteria: Adults 18-75 with advanced non-small cell lung cancer; ECOG 0-2; adequate organ function; within travel distance to Chicago site.
trials.exclusion_criteria: Prior treatment with the investigational drug; uncontrolled brain metastases; significant comorbid illness.
trials.visit_overview: Clinic visits every 2 weeks during the first 3 months, then monthly visits for follow-up assessments.
trials.location_summary: Chicago, IL 60611 (Downtown Cancer Center) within 5 miles of central Chicago.

trials.title: Phase III Immunotherapy vs Standard Care for Lung Cancer Near Chicago
trials.brief_summary: Randomized Phase III trial comparing immunotherapy plus chemotherapy versus standard chemotherapy for lung cancer.
trials.detailed_description: This multicenter Phase III trial compares a PD-1 inhibitor in combination with platinum-based chemotherapy against standard chemotherapy alone in adults with Stage IV non-small cell lung cancer. Chicago-area participants may enroll at a site within 15 miles of downtown.
trials.inclusion_criteria: Adults 18-80 with Stage IV non-small cell lung cancer; measurable disease; no prior systemic therapy for metastatic disease.
trials.exclusion_criteria: Active autoimmune disease requiring systemic treatment; prior PD-1 or PD-L1 inhibitor therapy.
trials.visit_overview: Infusions every 3 weeks with monthly imaging and optional telehealth toxicity checks between visits.
trials.location_summary: Suburban site in Oak Park, IL (approx. 12 miles from downtown Chicago) within 25 miles of Chicago, IL.

trials.title: Phase II Maintenance Therapy Trial for Lung Cancer Patients in Illinois
trials.brief_summary: Phase II trial of maintenance therapy following first-line treatment for non-small cell lung cancer.
trials.detailed_description: Enrolling adults who have completed first-line chemotherapy for advanced non-small cell lung cancer and achieved at least stable disease. Participants receive oral maintenance therapy with monthly follow-up visits.
trials.inclusion_criteria: Adults 30-85 with advanced NSCLC who have completed 4-6 cycles of platinum-based chemotherapy with at least stable disease.
trials.exclusion_criteria: Progressive disease after first-line chemotherapy; uncontrolled comorbidities.
trials.visit_overview: Monthly in-person visits with optional telehealth check-ins every 2 weeks.
trials.location_summary: Major academic center in Chicago, IL 60637; within 10 miles of downtown Chicago.

trials.title: Regional Lung Cancer Trial in Northern Illinois
trials.brief_summary: Interventional trial for lung cancer patients at a site approximately 40 miles from Chicago.
trials.detailed_description: This trial is conducted at a regional cancer center outside the immediate Chicago metro area and may require longer travel for participants living in the city.
trials.inclusion_criteria: Adults with advanced lung cancer able to travel to the northern Illinois regional site.
trials.exclusion_criteria: Inability to travel regularly to the study center.
trials.visit_overview: Biweekly infusion visits during the first 6 months followed by monthly visits.
trials.location_summary: Regional cancer center in northern Illinois approximately 40 miles from downtown Chicago.

trials.title: HER2+ Metastatic Breast Cancer Trial with Targeted Therapy in Los Angeles Area
trials.brief_summary: Phase II trial of a HER2-targeted agent for metastatic breast cancer near ZIP 90210.
trials.detailed_description: Adults with HER2-positive metastatic breast cancer will receive an investigational HER2-targeted therapy combined with standard backbone treatment. The main site is located in Beverly Hills, CA, with easy access from ZIP code 90210.
trials.inclusion_criteria: Adults 18-75 with HER2-positive metastatic breast cancer; prior trastuzumab allowed; measurable disease; residence within 50 miles of 90210 or willing to travel.
trials.exclusion_criteria: Symptomatic brain metastases; prior exposure to the investigational agent.
trials.visit_overview: Two in-person clinic visits per month, with optional telehealth toxicity checks between cycles.
trials.location_summary: Beverly Hills, CA 90210 (primary site) with satellite clinic in West Los Angeles within 10 miles.

trials.title: Comparative HER2+ Metastatic Breast Cancer Study in Southern California
trials.brief_summary: Phase III study comparing two HER2-targeted regimens for metastatic breast cancer, with a site near 90210.
trials.detailed_description: Randomized Phase III trial enrolling HER2-positive metastatic breast cancer patients at multiple Southern California sites, including a center within 20 miles of ZIP 90210. Participants receive one of two standard-of-care HER2-targeted regimens.
trials.inclusion_criteria: Adults with HER2-positive metastatic breast cancer; up to 1 prior line of therapy for metastatic disease allowed; ECOG 0-2.
trials.exclusion_criteria: Prior treatment with either of the study regimens in the metastatic setting.
trials.visit_overview: Infusion visits every 3 weeks plus monthly safety labs; approximately 3 in-person visits per month on average.
trials.location_summary: Los Angeles, CA (multiple sites within 50 miles of ZIP 90210).

trials.title: Featured Early-Stage Breast Cancer Trial Combining Radiation and Immunotherapy
trials.brief_summary: Phase II trial for early-stage breast cancer evaluating the safety of adding immunotherapy to standard radiation.
trials.detailed_description: This trial enrolls patients with early-stage, operable breast cancer after lumpectomy. Participants receive standard radiation therapy with or without an investigational immunotherapy agent.
trials.inclusion_criteria: Adults with early-stage, operable breast cancer who have undergone breast-conserving surgery.
trials.exclusion_criteria: History of autoimmune disease requiring systemic therapy; prior immunotherapy.
trials.visit_overview: Weekly radiation visits for 6 weeks with optional telehealth follow-up.
trials.location_summary: National multicenter study with major academic sites in large U.S. cities.

trials.title: Adjuvant Therapy Trial for Stage III Colorectal Cancer After Surgery
trials.brief_summary: Trial of adjuvant targeted therapy versus observation for Stage III colorectal cancer patients post-surgery.
trials.detailed_description: Adults with resected Stage III colorectal cancer are randomized to receive a targeted oral agent in addition to standard surveillance versus surveillance alone. The trial aims to improve disease-free survival after surgery.
trials.inclusion_criteria: Adults 18-80 with completely resected Stage III colorectal adenocarcinoma within the past 12 weeks; adequate organ function.
trials.exclusion_criteria: Residual disease on imaging; prior adjuvant chemotherapy or radiotherapy for current diagnosis.
trials.visit_overview: Clinic visits every 3 months with optional telehealth in-between for symptom review.
trials.location_summary: Multiple regional centers in the United States with both in-person and telehealth options.

trials.title: Chemotherapy Optimization Study for Stage III Colon Cancer
trials.brief_summary: Phase II study comparing shorter versus standard duration adjuvant chemotherapy for Stage III colon cancer.
trials.detailed_description: After curative surgery, adults with Stage III colon cancer receive either 3 or 6 months of standard adjuvant chemotherapy. The study evaluates non-inferiority of shorter treatment duration.
trials.inclusion_criteria: Adults with resected Stage III colon cancer within 8 weeks of surgery; eligible for oxaliplatin-based chemotherapy.
trials.exclusion_criteria: Rectal cancer; prior systemic chemotherapy for any cancer within 5 years.
trials.visit_overview: Biweekly chemotherapy infusions during the adjuvant period, then surveillance visits every 6 months.
trials.location_summary: Tertiary care cancer centers in the U.S. and Canada.

trials.title: Observational Study of Long-Term Outcomes in Pediatric Leukemia Survivors in New York
trials.brief_summary: Non-treatment observational study tracking long-term outcomes in children with leukemia, with telehealth options.
trials.detailed_description: This observational cohort study enrolls children and adolescents with a history of leukemia who are in remission. No chemotherapy or new treatment is given. Data are collected through clinic visits and telehealth to understand long-term health, school performance, and quality of life.
trials.inclusion_criteria: Children 5-17 years old with a prior diagnosis of leukemia in remission for at least 6 months; ability to attend visits in New York City or participate via telehealth.
trials.exclusion_criteria: Relapsed or refractory disease; planned intensive chemotherapy within the next 12 months.
trials.visit_overview: Clinic or telehealth visits every 6 months; optional remote surveys completed at home.
trials.location_summary: Children’s hospital in New York, NY with telehealth available to families within 100 miles.

trials.title: Pediatric Leukemia Chemotherapy De-escalation Trial in the Northeast
trials.brief_summary: Interventional trial testing reduced-intensity chemotherapy regimens for pediatric leukemia.
trials.detailed_description: Children with newly diagnosed acute lymphoblastic leukemia are randomized to receive standard or reduced-intensity chemotherapy protocols.
trials.inclusion_criteria: Children 1-18 years with newly diagnosed ALL treated at participating centers in the Northeast U.S.
trials.exclusion_criteria: Down syndrome; prior chemotherapy for leukemia.
trials.visit_overview: Intensive in-person chemotherapy schedule with multiple visits per month.
trials.location_summary: Pediatric oncology centers in New York City, NY and surrounding states.

trials.title: Prostate Cancer Active Surveillance Trial Near Atlanta (ZIP 30301)
trials.brief_summary: Observational study evaluating active surveillance strategies for low-risk prostate cancer.
trials.detailed_description: Men with low-risk localized prostate cancer are followed with regular PSA testing, imaging, and biopsies as needed. The study compares quality of life and outcomes across surveillance strategies.
trials.inclusion_criteria: Men 45-80 with low-risk localized prostate cancer eligible for active surveillance.
trials.exclusion_criteria: Prior radical prostatectomy or radiation therapy for prostate cancer.
trials.visit_overview: Clinic or telehealth visits every 6-12 months; more frequent visits if PSA rises.
trials.location_summary: Academic medical center in Atlanta, GA 30301 with community sites within 50 miles.

trials.title: Phase II Hormonal Therapy Trial for Advanced Prostate Cancer in Georgia
trials.brief_summary: Phase II interventional trial of intensified hormonal therapy for metastatic prostate cancer.
trials.detailed_description: Men with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer receive intensified hormonal therapy and are monitored for disease progression and side effects.
trials.inclusion_criteria: Men 40-85 with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer; adequate organ function.
trials.exclusion_criteria: Prior systemic therapy for metastatic prostate cancer.
trials.visit_overview: Monthly in-person clinic visits for injections and lab monitoring.
trials.location_summary: Regional cancer centers within 50 miles of ZIP 30301 (Atlanta, GA).

trials.title: Multi-Cancer Precision Medicine Trial with Comprehensive Travel Support in New York
trials.brief_summary: Basket trial for multiple solid tumors with generous travel assistance for participants traveling to New York City.
trials.detailed_description: Adults with advanced solid tumors of various types undergo genomic profiling and receive targeted therapy matched to their tumor mutations. The study provides substantial travel stipends and lodging support for eligible participants.
trials.inclusion_criteria: Adults with advanced solid tumors lacking standard treatment options; willingness to travel to New York, NY.
trials.exclusion_criteria: Uncontrolled brain metastases; serious concurrent illness.
trials.visit_overview: Clinic visits every 2-4 weeks with telehealth visits allowed between in-person appointments.
trials.location_summary: Major academic cancer center in New York, NY 10001 serving patients from across the region.

trials.title: Supportive Care Trial for Cancer-Related Fatigue with Travel Stipend
trials.brief_summary: Interventional supportive care study for adults with cancer experiencing fatigue, offering moderate travel reimbursement.
trials.detailed_description: Adults with any solid tumor or hematologic malignancy and significant fatigue will receive a structured exercise and counseling program. Travel stipends are provided for in-person visits.
trials.inclusion_criteria: Adults with any cancer diagnosis reporting moderate to severe fatigue.
trials.exclusion_criteria: Unstable cardiac disease; inability to safely exercise.
trials.visit_overview: Monthly in-person assessments combined with weekly telehealth coaching sessions.
trials.location_summary: Cancer rehabilitation clinic in Manhattan, New York, NY approximately 2 miles from ZIP 10001.

trials.title: Regional Oncology Trial with Limited Travel Reimbursement
trials.brief_summary: Multi-site oncology trial in the Northeast with small travel stipends for eligible participants.
trials.detailed_description: Adults with various solid tumors receive a novel supportive care intervention alongside standard treatment. Participating centers in New York state and neighboring regions offer limited travel reimbursement.
trials.inclusion_criteria: Adults with solid tumors receiving systemic therapy at participating centers in New York, New Jersey, or Connecticut.
trials.exclusion_criteria: Severe cognitive impairment; inability to provide consent.
trials.visit_overview: Monthly clinic visits for supportive care sessions and questionnaires.
trials.location_summary: Regional cancer centers across New York state, some within 100 miles of ZIP 10001.

trials.title: Completed Phase III Melanoma Immunotherapy Trial with Results Available
trials.brief_summary: Phase III trial of combination immunotherapy versus standard care for advanced melanoma; completed after 2020.
trials.detailed_description: This randomized Phase III trial compared combination checkpoint inhibition to standard single-agent immunotherapy in adults with unresectable or metastatic melanoma. Primary endpoint was overall survival. The trial has completed, and results are published.
trials.inclusion_criteria: Adults with unresectable Stage III or Stage IV melanoma; no prior systemic therapy for advanced disease.
trials.exclusion_criteria: Active autoimmune disease; prior CTLA-4 or PD-1/PD-L1 therapy.
trials.visit_overview: Infusions every 3 weeks for up to 2 years with regular safety and tumor assessments.
trials.location_summary: Global multicenter study including major melanoma centers in North America and Europe.

trials.title: Phase III Melanoma Vaccine Trial Completed in 2018
trials.brief_summary: Phase III study of a melanoma peptide vaccine that completed before 2020.
trials.detailed_description: This trial evaluated an investigational peptide vaccine in combination with standard adjuvant therapy in patients with high-risk resected melanoma.
trials.inclusion_criteria: Adults with resected high-risk Stage III melanoma; no evidence of disease at enrollment.
trials.visit_overview: Monthly injections during the first year with follow-up visits every 3 months.
trials.location_summary: Specialized melanoma centers in North America and Australia.
